name: mc_rng_seeding_seed_collision_full_year
description: AC-3 collision-safety fixture (audit HIGH). The cycle_id set for run_monte_carlo's seed is one full trading year at production cadence: 252 trading days x 390 market minutes = ~98,280 cycles. derive_cycle_mc_seed maps each YYYYMMDD_HHMM cycle_id (SHA-256 digest mod MC_SEED_MODULUS) to a numpy Generator seed. With the pre-fix MC_SEED_MODULUS = 2**31 the seed space is far too small for ~98k draws: the birthday bound predicts ~2.2 collisions/year and the 2024 trading-year cycle_id set produces a measured, deterministic collision. AC-3 widens the seed space to at least 2**64 (or the full SHA-256 digest via numpy SeedSequence), eliminating birthday-bound collisions. The test generates the cycle_id set programmatically (parameters below) so it is deterministic and RED-verifiable against pre-fix code.
provenance: The cycle_id format YYYYMMDD_HHMM and the production cadence (every market minute, 09:30-15:59 ET) are the daemon's real seeding inputs (alpha_bot_execution.py constructs the cycle_id as current_et.strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M')). No producer-computed value is stored -- the test asserts the SIZE of the seed space and the absence of collisions, both derivable.

contract.collision_free_at_scale: derive_cycle_mc_seed must map all ~98,280 cycle_ids of a trading year to DISTINCT seeds -- zero collisions.
contract.seed_space_width: MC_SEED_MODULUS (or the effective seed space) must be at least 2**64. 2**31 is too small (birthday bound: ~2.2 collisions/year at 98k cycles). The test asserts the modulus is >= 2**64 rather than == 2**64, so a full-digest / SeedSequence implementation that exposes an even larger space also passes.
contract.determinism_preserved: derive_cycle_mc_seed remains a pure deterministic function -- same cycle_id -> same seed across calls.
contract.comment_corrected: The MC_SEED_MODULUS comment must no longer claim '~98k distinct values/year with no collisions' for a 2**31 space -- that birthday-bound claim is false.

derivation: Birthday bound: expected collisions for n draws into M buckets ~= n^2 / (2M). n=98280, M=2**31 -> ~2.25 collisions/year (measured: 1 collision in the 2024 set). M=2**64 -> ~2.6e-10 collisions/year (effectively zero). The pre-fix RED marker asserts the measured >=1 collision exists at 2**31; the post-fix assertion is zero collisions plus a seed space >= 2**64.
